terms.definition: A JavaScript runtime built on V8 that executes JS outside the browser for servers, CLIs and tooling.
Node's single-threaded mechanism for scheduling non-blocking I/O and callbacks, enabling high concurrency.
Node's original module system using require and module.exports.
The standard JavaScript module system using import/export, enabled via "type": "module" or .mjs files.
The manifest declaring a Node project's dependencies, module type, and runnable scripts.
The object exposing environment variables to a Node process, commonly used for secrets and config.
